reforms.description: Portugal aims to improve its infrastructure to reduce input costs and enhance the entrepreneurial climate, which includes stimulating research and development spending that has been below par. The government is implementing measures to improve the climate for enterprise creation and innovation, focusing on reducing transaction costs and enhancing networks of small firms to stimulate R&D expenditure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving infrastructure and reducing costs for enterprises is expected to enhance R&D spending and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into practice as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it targets both infrastructure and enterprise climate but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government is also in the process of negotiating a medium-term strategic agreement with the social partners, the aim of which would be to ensure that macro-economic, social and structural policies are mutually consistent and capable of meeting Portugal's employment and convergence goals in an increasingly globalised economy.

reforms.description: Portugal is implementing steps to improve the administration of science and technology policy, particularly to increase research links between the public and private sectors and foster technology diffusion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing links between public and private sectors is expected to facilitate technology diffusion and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The steps have been actively taken to enhance research links.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it aims to improve the administration of science and technology policy.
reforms.source_quote: Portugal is implementing steps to improve the administration of science and technology policy, particularly to increase research links between the public and private sectors and foster technology diffusion.

reforms.description: Portugal established the Ministry for Science and Technology in 1995 and reorganised the National Board for Science and Technology Research (JNICT) to enhance flexibility in funding research and strengthen links with industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reorganization is expected to improve funding flexibility and strengthen industry links, fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The ministry and board have been operational since their establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated ministry and reorganization of the board represents a structural change in the governance of science and technology.
reforms.source_quote: In addition to creating the Ministry for Science and Technology in 1995, Portugal reorganised the National Board for Science and Technology Research (JNICT), to increase flexibility in funding research, as well as the Higher Council for Science and Technology to increase its links with industry.
